evals.prompt: Scan this Go service for observability gaps. The code is in examples/go/chi-basic/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Go/chi with zero instrumentation and listing specific missing signals

discovery: Report identifies Go as the language
Report identifies chi (go-chi/chi/v5) as the HTTP framework
Report identifies main.go as the entry point
Report identifies 6 HTTP routes: GET /health, GET /tasks, GET /tasks/{id}, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/{id}, DELETE /tasks/{id}
coverage_gaps: Report notes no OTel SDK initialization exists
Report notes no go.opentelemetry.io dependencies in go.mod
Report notes missing otelhttp or otelchi middleware for HTTP server spans
Report notes missing http.server.request.duration histogram (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing HTTP request rate visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate visibility via span status codes (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions otelhttp or otelchi as the auto-instrumentation to add

evals.prompt: Assess the observability readiness of my Express app at examples/node/express-basic/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Node.js/Express with zero instrumentation and listing specific missing signals

discovery: Report identifies Node.js as the language
Report identifies Express as the HTTP framework from package.json
Report identifies app.js as the entry point
Report identifies 6 HTTP routes: GET /health, GET /tasks, GET /tasks/:id, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/:id, DELETE /tasks/:id
coverage_gaps: Report notes no OTel SDK initialization exists
Report notes no @opentelemetry/* packages in package.json
Report notes missing @opentelemetry/instrumentation-http for HTTP server spans
Report notes missing @opentelemetry/instrumentation-express for Express-specific spans
Report notes missing http.server.request.duration histogram (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing HTTP request rate visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate visibility via span status codes (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions @opentelemetry/instrumentation-http and @opentelemetry/instrumentation-express as packages to add

evals.prompt: Analyze this Flask service for observability gaps. The code is at examples/python/flask-basic/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Python/Flask with zero instrumentation and listing specific missing signals

discovery: Report identifies Python as the language
Report identifies Flask as the HTTP framework from pyproject.toml
Report identifies app.py as the entry point
Report identifies 6 HTTP routes: GET /health, GET /tasks, GET /tasks/<task_id>, POST /tasks, PATCH /tasks/<task_id>, DELETE /tasks/<task_id>
coverage_gaps: Report notes no OTel SDK initialization exists
Report notes no opentelemetry packages in pyproject.toml
Report notes missing opentelemetry-instrumentation-flask for HTTP server spans
Report notes missing http.server.request.duration histogram (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing HTTP request rate visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate visibility via span status codes (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions opentelemetry-instrumentation-flask as the package to add

evals.prompt: Scan this Go key-value store service for observability gaps. The code is in examples/go/kvstore/
evals.expected_output: Report identifying Go/stdlib ServeMux with zero instrumentation, multi-package structure, and listing specific missing signals including filesystem and background worker coverage

discovery: Report identifies Go as the language
Report identifies stdlib net/http (http.ServeMux) as the HTTP router (not chi or gorilla)
Report identifies cmd/kvstore-server/main.go as the entry point
Report identifies the multi-package structure: cmd/kvstore-server (entry point) and kvstore/ (library)
Report identifies HTTP routes: GET/PUT/DELETE /kv/{key} and GET /search?word=
Report identifies filesystem persistence in store.go (os.WriteFile, os.Remove, filepath.WalkDir)
Report identifies background goroutine: indexLoop for async word indexing
coverage_gaps: Report notes no OTel SDK initialization exists
Report notes no go.opentelemetry.io dependencies in go.mod
Report notes missing otelhttp middleware for HTTP server spans
Report notes missing http.server.request.duration histogram (RED: Duration)
Report notes missing HTTP request rate visibility (RED: Rate)
Report notes missing error rate visibility via span status codes (RED: Errors)
Report notes no OTLP exporter is configured
Report notes filesystem I/O (persistAsync, loadFromDisk) has no tracing coverage
Report notes LRU eviction events have no observability
anti_patterns: Report notes no anti-patterns (since there is no instrumentation to misconfigure)
recommendation: Report specifically mentions otelhttp as the auto-instrumentation to add for the ServeMux
Report suggests custom spans or metrics for filesystem persistence and eviction as optional enhancements
